name: Berry Global Group, Inc. (Acquired by Amcor plc)
lede: Berry runs three capability centers across three Indian cities. Bengaluru, Tumkur and Mundra, covering CoE and manufacturing, built over four years.
insight: Search elsewhere and you will usually find one Berry office in India. We map three, across three cities, each tagged by center type and function. Knowing the whole footprint is what turns a name on a list into an account you can plan against.
metaTitle: Berry GCC in India: Centers, Footprint & Leaders | Bamboo Reports
metaDescription: Berry runs 3 verified GCC centers across 3 Indian cities (Bengaluru, Tumkur, Mundra), covering CoE and manufacturing. See the full India footprint, org map and contacts on Bamboo Reports.

leaders.departments: Finance, IT, HR, GCC leadership and Procurement

titles.title: Managing Director -India , Vice President -General Manager -India & Indonesia

about.description: Berry's India footprint covers Bengaluru, Tumkur and Mundra, spanning CoE and manufacturing. The centers deliver R&D and ER&D for the group's global operations.

bandCopy: Exact center locations, per-center headcount, the technology stack and all 25 decision-makers, in one profile.

faq.q: How many GCC centers does Berry operate in India?
faq.a: Berry operates 3 active Global Capability Center sites in India.

faq.q: Which Indian cities does Berry have GCC centers in?
faq.a: Berry's India GCC footprint spans Bengaluru, Tumkur and Mundra.

faq.q: What types of GCC centers does Berry run in India?
faq.a: Berry runs CoE and manufacturing centers in India.

faq.q: How many people work at Berry's India GCCs?
faq.a: Berry's India centers employ in the 201 to 500 range. The exact aggregate and the headcount at each site are in the full profile.

faq.q: Who leads Berry's GCC in India?
faq.a: Bamboo Reports tracks 25 leaders at Berry's India centers, across Finance, IT, HR, GCC leadership and Procurement. Names and contact details open with a free account.

sources: company filings, LinkedIn, NASSCOM and Bamboo Reports' primary research
